blocks.content: She always noticed. She never crowded it — just waited until the moment was right, which was when he was helping her clear the dishes.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: You want to talk about it or you want to leave it?
They did the dishes. The radio was on low. Kit handed her plates and she stacked them.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I think I did something I shouldn't have.
His mom didn't react, just kept stacking.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I don't know. Maybe. Not on purpose.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: The not-on-purpose ones are sometimes worse.
He handed her the last bowl.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: Kit. You can like a lot of people in your life. That's normal, that's good. But the ones who actually know you — the ones who've seen the whole of you and stayed anyway — you don't get many of those.
He looked at the dish towel in his hands.
She put the last dish away and turned off the kitchen light on her way out. Kit stood at the sink and looked at his reflection in the dark window.
